'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Jōgan era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}})

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nasu Yuzen-jinja]][[ja:那須温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nasu Yuzen-jinja]][[ja:那須温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nasu Yuzen-jinja]][[ja:那須温泉神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q111076400]] Wikidata name: Nasu Yuzen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 那須温泉神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image = [[File:Nasuyuzen.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}{{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}湯本182 |地図 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}}、{{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona}}、{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hodanbetsu-no-mikoto}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Jōgan era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}})

'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Jōgan era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}})

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nasu Yuzen-jinja]][[ja:那須温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nasu Yuzen-jinja]][[ja:那須温泉神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nasu Yuzen-jinja]][[ja:那須温泉神社]]
{{Infobox religious building |名称 = 那須温泉神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image = [[File:Nasuyuzen.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}{{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}湯本182 |地図 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}}、{{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona}}、{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hodanbetsu-no-mikoto}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Jōgan era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}})
<gallery widths="200"> File:Nasuyuzen3.jpg|Mitate Shrine (見立神社) File:Nasuyuzen4.jpg|Sorei Shrine (祖霊社) File:Nasuyuzen2.jpg|Nine-tailed Inari Shrine (九尾稲荷神社) </gallery>

{{Infobox religious building |名称 = 那須温泉神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image = [[File:Nasuyuzen.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}{{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}湯本182 |地図 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}}、{{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona}}、{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hodanbetsu-no-mikoto}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Jōgan era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}})

{{Infobox religious building |名称 = 那須温泉神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image = [[File:Nasuyuzen.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}{{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}湯本182 |地図 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}}、{{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona}}、{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hodanbetsu-no-mikoto}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Jōgan era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}})
<gallery widths="200"> File:Nasuyuzen3.jpg|Mitate Shrine (見立神社) File:Nasuyuzen4.jpg|Sorei Shrine (祖霊社) File:Nasuyuzen2.jpg|Nine-tailed Inari Shrine (九尾稲荷神社) </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki}} * {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox}} * {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae}}

'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town|1=Nasu, Tochigi}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629|1=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641|1=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Q124344147|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869|1=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima|1=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi|1=Nasu no Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō|1=Futarasan shrine}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era|1=Jōkyō}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686|1=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave|1=Suikinkutsu}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki|1=Sessho-seki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae|1=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox|1=Nine-tailed fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae|1=Tamamo-no-Mae}})

{{Infobox religious building |名称 = 那須温泉神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|image = [[File:Nasuyuzen.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}{{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town|1=Nasu, Tochigi}}湯本182 |地図 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}、{{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona|1=Sukunabikona}}、{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hodanbetsu-no-mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town|1=Nasu, Tochigi}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629|1=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641|1=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Q124344147|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869|1=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima|1=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi|1=Nasu no Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō|1=Futarasan shrine}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era|1=Jōkyō}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686|1=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave|1=Suikinkutsu}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki|1=Sessho-seki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae|1=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox|1=Nine-tailed fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae|1=Tamamo-no-Mae}})
<gallery widths="200"> File:Nasuyuzen3.jpg|Mitate Shrine (見立神社) File:Nasuyuzen4.jpg|Sorei Shrine (祖霊社) File:Nasuyuzen2.jpg|Nine-tailed Inari Shrine (九尾稲荷神社) </gallery>

The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era|1=Jōkyō}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686|1=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave|1=Suikinkutsu}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>

'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town|1=Nasu, Tochigi}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629|1=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641|1=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Q124344147|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869|1=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima|1=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi|1=Nasu no Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō|1=Futarasan shrine}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era|1=Jōkyō}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686|1=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave|1=Suikinkutsu}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki|1=Sessho-seki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae|1=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox|1=Nine-tailed fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae|1=Tamamo-no-Mae}})
<gallery widths="200"> File:Nasuyuzen3.jpg|Mitate Shrine (見立神社) File:Nasuyuzen4.jpg|Sorei Shrine (祖霊社) File:Nasuyuzen2.jpg|Nine-tailed Inari Shrine (九尾稲荷神社) </gallery>

{{Infobox religious building |名称 = 那須温泉神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|image = [[File:Nasuyuzen.jpg|250px]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}{{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town|1=Nasu, Tochigi}}湯本182 |地図 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}、{{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona|1=Sukunabikona}}、{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hodanbetsu-no-mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Nasu Onsen Shrine''' (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Nasu, Tochigi|en|Nasu, Tochigi|WD=Q636416|lt=Nasu Town|1=Nasu, Tochigi}}.
== Overview == It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} (reigned: {{ill|629|en|629|WD=Q33258|lt=629|1=629}} – {{ill|641|en|641|WD=Q31676|lt=641|1=641}}).<ref name="jre">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://sightseeing.jrnets.co.jp/detail/spot/4418.html|title=Nasu Onsen Shrine (那須温泉神社【なすゆぜんじんじゃ】)|publisher={{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}|work=観光スポット・イベント情報|accessdate=2022-03-04}}</ref> In ancient times, in the 11th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|上元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|1=Q124344147|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124344147}} ({{ill|869|en|869|WD=Q23917|lt=869|1=869}}), the shrine was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071127}} and its name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}’s {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, during the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|en|Battle of Yashima|WD=Q2117322|lt=Battle of Yashima|1=Battle of Yashima}}, when {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|en|Nasu no Yoichi|WD=Q2625800|lt=Nasu Yoichi|1=Nasu no Yoichi}} took aim at the fan’s target, he prayed to Nasu Onsen Shrine, exclaiming, “Namu {{ill|Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja|八幡大菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Great Bodhisattva|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, and beyond that, the divine spirit of our country, {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Gongen of Nikkō|1=Futarasan shrine}}{{ill|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|en|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|WD=Q10946254|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine}}, Nasu Onsen Daimyōjin, may you let my arrow strike dead center of that fan.” His successful shot brought him great renown, and he became the head of {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}; after his triumphant return, in gratitude for the divine favor, he donated a grand shrine building and offered items such as the kabura arrow, the toad-eye arrow, the征矢, a cypress fan, and a torii. The local people collectively enshrined the Onsen Shrine, and on June 19 of the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era|1=Jōkyō}} ({{ill|1686|en|1686|WD=Q7664|lt=1686|1=1686}}), it was conferred the Rank of {{ill|Shoichii|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii|1=Senior First Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123258}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nasu-yuzen.jp/sp/|title=宗教法人 那須温泉神社|publisher=Nasu Onsen Shrine|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref> Following Yoichi’s example, many continue to visit for prayers for certain victory.<ref name="jre"/>
Within the shrine grounds are the three torii contributed by Yoichi, as well as the {{ill|Suikinkutsu|en|Suikinkutsu|WD=Q1196263|lt=Water Koto Cave|1=Suikinkutsu}} and the {{ill|Sazare Stone|ja|さざれ石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazare Stone|1=sazare-ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11267921}}.<ref name="jre"/>
Behind the shrine, there is a {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sesshōseki|1=Sessho-seki}} which is said to be the petrified form of {{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae|1=Tamamo-no-Mae}}.
== Sub-shrines == : '''Atago Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hikatsuri no Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} : '''Mitate Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amanoyowane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, Kano Saburō Yukihiro : '''Sorei Shrine''' : '''Kotohira Shrine''' : '''Smallpox Shrine''' : '''Shinmei Shrine''' : '''Mountain Shrine''' : '''Wakaji Shrine''' : '''Tenma Tenjin''' 祭神 - {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|lt=Lord Sugawara|lt_ja=公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} : '''Nine-tailed Inari Shrine''' 祭神 - {{ill|Nine-tailed fox|en|Nine-tailed fox|WD=Q15063121|lt=Nine-tailed Fox|1=Nine-tailed fox}} ({{ill|Tamamo-no-Mae|en|Tamamo-no-Mae|WD=Q1327792|lt=Tamamo-no-Mae|1=Tamamo-no-Mae}})
